element_introduction_order.element: Special move inputs (shown after bonus rounds)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic depth; CPU controls switching in single-player until player can respond
Rhythm-based tension break; rapid button input challenge
Escalating opponent difficulty toward final boss Geese Howard

element_introduction_order.element: Final boss encounters (Billy Kane, Geese Howard)

typical_relief_triggers: Arm wrestling bonus round (breaks match tension with rhythm mini-game)
typical_tension_triggers: Two-lane switching by CPU (forces reactive repositioning)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament bracket structure provides escalating tension with periodic relief via bonus round mini-games; match-to-match progression builds narrative pressure toward final boss confrontation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Two-lane stages allow strategic depth; background/foreground positioning is non-mandatory but tactically relevant

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundation building; early tournament matches teach basic mechanics and special move inputs via post-bonus-round instruction display
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tournament bracket deepens; player gains mastery of two-lane positioning and special move timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalated opponent AI; final bosses (Billy Kane, Geese Howard) represent peak difficulty and narrative climax
notable_spikes: Introduction of two-lane mechanics (creates new positional learning curve)
Final boss encounters with Geese Howard (narrative payoff, highest difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Many stages feature a background row and foreground row; players can switch between lanes at any time (multiplayer) or reactively (single-player, waiting for CPU to switch first). Adds spatial strategy beyond standard left-right fighting game movement.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage-dependent (specific stages not named in source)

notable_level_patterns.description: After every other match, arm wrestling mini-game interrupts tournament flow. Requires rapid button input to win; provides rhythmic break from combat.
Single-player mode structured as tournament leading to Geese Howard confrontation. Escalating opponent difficulty; narrative revenge arc building throughout.
